summary: Moved a large contiguous block of global .data out of asm/data.s and into the assembly files that own the corresponding code, covering Melee menu/item/text assembly and MSL library files up to the MetroTRK boundary. The PR was primarily an organization/splitting change: asm/data.s lost about 19.7k lines, while per-module files such as asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s, asm/melee/text_4.s, many asm/melee/mn/*.s files, and asm/MSL/{math,printf,strtoul}.s gained local .section .data blocks containing labels, constants, pointer tables, jump tables, and string-like hex data.
what_changed: Removed nearly all content from asm/data.s, deleting 19727 lines from the monolithic data file.
Added .section .data blocks to module-local assembly files so each file now carries the data associated with that translation unit or library object.
Moved large data ranges into asm/melee/it/code_80266F3C.s and asm/melee/text_4.s, which together account for most of the added lines.

Split menu-related data into many asm/melee/mn files, including mnname, mncharsel, mndiagram, mnsoundtest, mninfobonus, mnstagesel, mnsnap, mndatadel, mnvibration, mncount, mnevent, mnitemsw, mnsound, mnhyaku, mndeflicker, mninfo, mnlanguage, mnstagesw, and mngallery.
Added MSL library data to asm/MSL/math.s, asm/MSL/printf.s, and asm/MSL/strtoul.s rather than leaving it in the shared data blob.
Preserved low-level labels and addresses such as lbl_80400430, lbl_8040043C, jtbl_80400508, jtbl_8040058C, jtbl_80400610, lbl_80400728, lbl_80400770, lbl_80400774, and lbl_80400778.

Preserved jump-table structure in the destination files by moving .4byte entries that reference local code labels, for example printf jump tables targeting lbl_80324ED8, lbl_80325168, and lbl_80325694.
smart_moves: The split keeps data near the assembly code that references it, which makes later decompilation and symbol recovery easier than working from one enormous asm/data.s file.
The change appears intentionally mechanical: most files have a single hunk adding a .data section, which reduces semantic risk and makes review/build verification focus on binary layout preservation.
MSL data was split into MSL-owned files rather than mixed with game menu data, maintaining library boundaries useful for future replacement with C or known library source.
Jump tables and callback/pointer tables were preserved as raw .4byte label references, avoiding premature typing or renaming where evidence was not yet available.
Alignment directives such as .balign 4 and .balign 1 were carried into the moved data blocks where shown in the diff, protecting exact section layout.
decomp_lessons: Before converting a module to C, first relocate its .data from the monolithic data file into the module's asm file; this creates a clearer translation-unit boundary.
When splitting data, preserve original label names, ordering, alignment, and NULL padding exactly unless there is stronger evidence for a semantic rename or typed structure.
Jump tables should travel with the function/file that uses them, especially when their entries reference nearby code labels; this makes later switch reconstruction easier.
Library objects such as MSL math, printf, and strtoul should have their data separated from game code data to avoid confusing SDK/library reconstruction with Melee-specific reconstruction.
Large data-only PRs can be valuable even without new C code because they reduce global coupling and make subsequent matching work more searchable and modular.
naming_conventions: Kept autogenerated address-based labels in the form lbl_80xxxxxx for unknown data symbols.
Kept jump-table labels in the form jtbl_80xxxxxx for tables of branch targets.
Retained existing assembly file/module names such as mnname, mncharsel, mndiagram, mnsoundtest, code_80266F3C, text_3, and text_4.
Used uppercase hex in some label suffixes and references where already present, such as lbl_8040043C and func_80325F18; no evidence of semantic renaming in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move data by inserting .section .data into the owning asm file while keeping the section transitions to .sdata, .sdata2, or .sbss intact afterward.
Maintain .balign directives around mixed-width data, especially where byte fields and word-aligned structures coexist.
Represent unknown pointers and zeroes with .4byte NULL consistently in moved data.
Represent jump tables as ordered .4byte lists of code labels and keep the terminal NULL or padding entries when present.
Avoid changing data encodings during a split: many strings remain as .4byte hex words rather than being rewritten as ASCII directives in the shown diff.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided slice, so there is no direct reviewer rationale or requested follow-up to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert mn*.s files to C after their data was split from asm/data.s.
Search for later semantic renames of lbl_80400430, lbl_8040043C, jtbl_80400508, jtbl_8040058C, and jtbl_80400610 in MSL printf work.
Search for later PRs that continue splitting .data after the MetroTRK boundary.
Check whether code_80266F3C.s data was later typed or moved into item-related C headers.
Compare this split with later .rodata, .sdata, or .bss split PRs for reusable section-order tactics.
